findings.title: Production container runs as root
findings.description: The runtime stage (debian:bookworm-slim) has no USER directive. The loveliness binary runs as root (UID 0) inside the container.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker exploits a vulnerability in LadybugDB's CGo layer (e.g., the known segfault in ALL SHORTEST paths) or a future RCE in the Cypher engine. 2. Code execution lands as root inside the container. 3. Root access enables container escape techniques (kernel exploits, mount namespace abuse), reading /proc/1/environ for secrets, and pivoting to other containers on the same host.
findings.impact: Container compromise gives full root privileges. Increases blast radius of any RCE from 'unprivileged process' to 'root in container'.
findings.recommendation:
Add a non-root user to the Dockerfile runtime stage:

```dockerfile
RUN useradd -r -s /bin/false loveliness
USER loveliness
```

Ensure the data directory is writable by this user. Place this after the COPY and ldconfig steps but before ENTRYPOINT.
findings.verification: self-verified — confirmed no USER directive in Dockerfile runtime stage (line 17 onwards)
